response.justification: Wei Chen brings 15+ years of polyglot full-stack experience with solid alignment on TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, Docker, and Kubernetes, and critically includes current AI agent and MCP (Model Context Protocol) framework work at re:cinq that maps directly to the role's AI agent development requirement. A strong testing and craftsmanship culture is evidenced by published testing libraries, TDD workshops, and conference talks. Key gaps exist: Nest.js and Vitest (both primary stack items) are not mentioned, Kotlin is absent despite being a secondary requirement, and Python does not appear anywhere. Additionally, the resume lists contact details (website, GitHub, LinkedIn) that appear to belong to a different person, which warrants clarification before proceeding.
strengths: Current AI agent and MCP infrastructure work at re:cinq directly satisfies the AI agent development requirement — a differentiating and hard-to-screen-for skill
15+ years of full-stack depth across TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS CDK, Docker, and Kubernetes at Staff/Senior level across multiple product companies
Demonstrated testing culture via TDD practices, open-source testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and community workshops — aligns with the role's quality and code-review expectations
concerns: Nest.js and Vitest — both listed as primary stack — are absent from the resume, creating uncertainty about day-one contribution speed on the core framework
No mention of Kotlin (secondary stack) or Python (required multi-language proficiency), leaving notable gaps against the stated technical requirements

Resume header names 'Wei Chen' but all contact details (website, GitHub, GitLab, LinkedIn) resolve to a different person, raising an authenticity or copy-paste issue that must be resolved before advancing
